- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-5-1-14-spirit-magic-list-part-3` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0021-0024` [uncertainty, limitation, requirement, identity] terms `spirit, same, control, requires` statement `It is the same as _Control Spirit_ in that it requires a three-hour ritual, only one type of spirit can be controlled at a time, and it is...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0021-0030` [uncertainty, identity] terms `command, spirit, however, you` statement `However, if you command a spirit to do something that is too contrary to its true nature, the spirit may disappear after carrying out the command.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0021-0021` [method, identity] terms `spell, control, used, summon` statement `This is a spell used to summon and control lesser spirits, and is an advanced _Control Spirit_ spell.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0021-0025` [provenance, identity] terms `spirit, however, biggest, difference` statement `However, the biggest difference from _Control Spirit_ is that it can ignore the spirit's will and force the caster's commands.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0021-0018` [unlabeled] terms `area, one, lesser, spirit` statement `Area=One lesser spirit Duration=Permanent` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.84-95` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.84-95` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-5-1-14-spirit-magic-list-part-3.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-5-1-15-holy-magic-and-the-priest-skill` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0022-0036` [method, identity] terms `holy, prayers, runes, used` statement `**Holy prayers** are runes used to contact the great beings, the _gods_ , and use that power as a source to perform various miracles.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0022-0057` [limitation, relationship, requirement] terms `those, priest, skill, must` statement `Those with the priest skill must choose only _one_ god to worship.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0022-0043` [limitation, method, attribute] terms `addition, holy, magic, has` statement `In addition, each holy magic has _special_ spells that can only be used by believers of a specific god.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0022-0052` [identity] terms `there, restrictions, armor, weapons` statement `There are no restrictions on armor or weapons to use the priest skill, and priests can cast magic simply by chanting holy prayers.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0022-0050` [requirement] terms `game, masters, should, not` statement `Game masters should _not_ allow characters to become priests of wicked gods.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.96-98` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.96-98` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-5-1-15-holy-magic-and-the-priest-skill.md`

- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-5-1-16-basic-holy-magic-list-part-2` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0024-0082` [unlabeled] terms `myrii, priests, receive, assistance` statement `Priests of Myrii can receive Myrii's assistance by offering a song in holy prayer to Myrii.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0024-0083` [provenance] terms `spell, caster, basically, takes` statement `Basically, this spell takes effect on anything within the reach of the caster's singing voice, but the caster can also exclude any target from being targeted by the...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0024-0084` [quantitative] terms `those, under, influence, spell` statement `Those under the influence of this spell gain a +2 bonus to attack power.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.99-108` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `possible, unknown`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.99-108` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-5-1-16-basic-holy-magic-list-part-2.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-5-3-spellsongs` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0025-0021` [uncertainty, limitation, identity] terms `resistance, rolls, against, spellsongs` statement `Resistance rolls against spellsongs are generally made only once at the beginning of the song (depending on the spellsong, they may be on the fourth round).`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0025-0024` [limitation, method, requirement] terms `instrument, must, unique, bard` statement `This instrument must be unique to each bard, and the spellsong cannot be used when playing another instrument.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0025-0016` [limitation, attribute] terms `other, hand, even, grassrunners` statement `On the other hand, even grassrunners who have no magical background can use spellsongs, because they only need to prepare the superficial form.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0025-0034` [function, identity] terms `types, effects, spellsongs, shown` statement `The types and effects of spellsongs are shown below.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0025-0012` [method] terms `using, spellsongs, does, not` statement `Using spellsongs does _not_ consume a bard's mental power.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.109-113` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.109-113` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-5-3-spellsongs.md`

- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-8-1-experience-points` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0030-0001` [identity, attribute] terms `experience, points, given, characters` statement `**Experience points** are points given to characters as an indicator of how much experience they have gained as a result of their adventures.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0030-0020` [temporal, quantitative, function] terms `experience, points, his, gains` statement `If he gains 5000 experience points again on his next adventure, he can now use his 5500 experience points to advance.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0030-0017` [uncertainty] terms `may, seem, strange, carry` statement `It may seem strange to carry over _experience_ , but think of it as cutting off the adventurer's growth by the`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0030-0002` [method, identity] terms `character, growth, done, using` statement `**Character growth** is done using these experience points.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0030-0024` [provenance, identity] terms `you, grow, experiences, haven` statement `This is because you can't grow from experiences you haven't had.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.130-135` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, might, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.130-135` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-8-1-experience-points.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-9-2-should-you-use-a-target-score-check-or-a-difficulty-check` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0031-0003` [identity] terms `problem, target, score, difficulty` statement `The problem is the target score or difficulty.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0031-0001` [requirement] terms `score, make, success, roll` statement `To make a success roll, you must decide on the _skill to be used_ , the _relevant ability score_ , and the _target score or difficulty_ .`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0031-0019` [limitation, quantitative, identity] terms `success, even, master, best` statement `It is best not to expect success at pro level.| |18|11|Even a master cannot guarantee success.| |19|12|You can't blame a master even if they fail.| |20|13|50-50 for masters.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0031-0046` [negative-evidence, relationship] terms `you, really, want, include` statement `If you really want to include traps with different difficulties, then when the difficulty changes, just nonchalantly say _Okay, make a success roll_ without telling`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0031-0018` [uncertainty, quantitative, identity] terms `you, master, pro, failure` statement `At pro level, failure is quite possible.| |15|8|If youŌĆÖre a master, youŌĆÖll never fail, but if youŌĆÖre a pro, success is 50-50.| |16|9|Even if youŌĆÖre a master, there...` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.135-138` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.135-138` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-9-2-should-you-use-a-target-score-check-or-a-difficulty-check.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-10-3-surprise-attacks` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0032-0016` [limitation, quantitative, identity, attribute] terms `etc, evasion, speed, you` statement `|**Table 10-1: Attack Power and Evasion Speed Modifers**|| |---|---| |Full movement (Evasion speed only)|-4| |CanŌĆÖt see opponent (in the dark, invisible enemy, etc.)<br> However, if alternative senses (smell,...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0032-0008` [method, relationship, requirement] terms `character, used, spell, they` statement `They must _not_ change things based on the circumstances, such as attacking with fangs if the character used the spell _Counter Magic_ to increase their resistance, or breathing...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0032-0018` [uncertainty, negative-evidence] terms `you, encountering, monster, one` statement `When encountering a monster, one of you may notice the other without them noticing you, allowing you to perform a **surprise attack** .`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0032-0017` [quantitative, function, identity] terms `example, shown, table, attack` statement `An example is shown in Table 10-1: Attack Power and Evasion Speed Modifiers.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0032-0012` [identity] terms `such, cases, penalty, sometimes` statement `In such cases, a penalty (sometimes a bonus) is given to an adventurer's attack power or evasion speed, depending on the situation.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.138-142` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.138-142` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-10-3-surprise-attacks.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-11-2-dark-magic-part-1` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0033-0013` [limitation, identity] terms `priests, narrow, sense, only` statement `In a narrow sense, only the priests of Phalaris are sometimes called dark priests.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0033-0012` [identity] terms `dark, rune, master, who` statement `A rune master who uses **dark magic** is called a **dark priest** .`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0033-0014` [comparison, identity] terms `believers, wicked, gods, other` statement `This is because believers in wicked gods other than Phalaris are rarely seen.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.142-148` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, might, possible, unknown`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.142-148` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-11-2-dark-magic-part-1.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-11-2-dark-magic-part-2` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0034-0001` [method, identity] terms `here, list, special, dark` statement `Here is a list of special dark magic that can be used uniquely by each wicked god.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0034-0002` [unlabeled] terms `magic, basic, dark, priests` statement `Priests of wicked gods can use basic holy magic, basic dark magic, _and_ special dark magic created by their god.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0034-0005` [quantitative] terms `area, one, duration, rounds` statement `Area=One Duration=18 rounds` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.142-148` in every bullet_text; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.142-148` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-11-2-dark-magic-part-2.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-11-3-dragon-roar-magic` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0035-0004` [method, comparison, identity] terms `dragon, roar, magic, runes` statement `**Dragon roar magic** are runes used by those who worship dragons as guardians rather than gods.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0035-0014` [mechanism] terms `repeated, training, users, dragon` statement `Through repeated training, users of dragon roar magic can master the physical, mental, and magical transcendental abilities of dragons.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0035-0011` [limitation, attribute] terms `those, who, believe, dragons` statement `Those who believe in dragons have only one goal, _to be reborn as a dragon_ .`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0035-0003` [identity] terms `magic, you, even, silence` statement `Even if _Silence_ or _Mute_ spirit magic is cast on you, you can still use dragon roar magic!`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0035-0008` [provenance, identity] terms `among, said, there, users` statement `It is said that there are users of dragon roar magic among uncivilized savage tribes and among the greater species of lizardmen, an intelligent race born from the...` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.149-153` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.149-153` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-11-3-dragon-roar-magic.md`

- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-12-2-rules-for-poison-illness-and-infection-part-1` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0037-0026` [uncertainty, identity] terms `addition, wound, may, become` statement `In addition, a wound may become **infected** , which is treated as an illness.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0037-0034` [uncertainty, function] terms `unknown, spirit, life, heals` statement `The unknown spirit of life heals illness by showing other spirits in the body how to achieve balance.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0037-0048` [uncertainty] terms `however, not, possible, drive` statement `However, itŌĆÖs _not_ possible to drive off a poison thatŌĆÖs _already_ entered the body.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0037-0042` [method, attribute] terms `magic, poisons, entered, body` statement `Poisons that have entered the body can be cleared using specific antidotes (if any), the holy magic _Cure Poison_ or _Refresh_ , or the spirit magic _Restore Health_...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0037-0025` [identity] terms `due, one, poison, other` statement `One is due to **poison** and the other is due to **illness** .` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.159-167` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible, suggest, unknown`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.159-167` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-12-2-rules-for-poison-illness-and-infection-part-1.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-12-2-rules-for-poison-illness-and-infection-part-2` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0038-0001` [identity] terms `illness, details, expressed, follows` statement `Illness details are expressed as follows.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0038-0004` [unlabeled] terms `infection, power, moderate` statement `Infection Power=Moderate`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0038-0007` [uncertainty] terms `some, people, may, experience` statement `Some people may experience diarrhea symptoms or joint pain.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.159-169` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible, unknown`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.159-169` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-12-2-rules-for-poison-illness-and-infection-part-2.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-1-types-of-monsters` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0039-0001` [quantitative, identity] terms `beasts, magical, there, types` statement `There are 12 types of monsters in Alecrast: **fae** , **youma** , **spirits** , **titans** , **mythical beasts** , **magical beasts** , **animals** , **plants** , **undead** ,...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0039-0002` [identity] terms `based, types, set, laverna` statement `This is based on the types set by Laverna, the famous witch of Orphan, who compiled the _Natural History of Alecrast_ .`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0039-0004` [identity] terms `certain, spirits, involved, activities` statement `certain that spirits are involved in the activities of life itself.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.170-172` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.170-172` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-1-types-of-monsters.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-1-how-to-read-the-monster-catalog` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0040-0005` [quantitative, identity] terms `there, monsters, alecrast, not` statement `There are also monsters in Alecrast that do not belong to any of the 12 types.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0040-0008` [quantitative] terms `points, resistance, none, monster` statement `Monster Level=1 Rarity=3 Agility=15 Movement Speed=20 Number=One to around ten Frequency=Frequent Intellect=Animal Reaction=Neutral to friendly Attack Points=Fangs: 8 (1) Strike Points=4 Evasion Points=9 (2) Defense Points=4 Life Points/Resistance=12/7...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0040-0006` [unlabeled] terms `examples, include, lycanthropes, red` statement `Examples of this include lycanthropes and red caps, where humans become monsters due to infectious diseases.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0040-0007` [identity] terms `monster, data, written, following` statement `Monster data is written in the following format.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.172-175` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, might`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.172-175` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-1-how-to-read-the-monster-catalog.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-2-fae` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0041-0015` [identity] terms `world, live, fairies, representative` statement `Fairies are a representative race of fae, and many of them still live in the fairy world, and rarely come to the material world, the world we live...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0041-0017` [provenance, identity] terms `stories, often, told, humans` statement `Stories are often told of humans who inadvertently wandered into the fairy world from these places.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0041-0016` [relationship, identity, attribute] terms `world, however, there, some` statement `However, there are some places in the material world that have contact with the fairy world.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.176-180` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible, suggest, uncertain, unknown`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.176-180` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-2-fae.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-4-spirits` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0042-0009` [identity] terms `they, appear, mad, spirits` statement `When they appear as mad spirits, there are two actions they can take.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0042-0008` [identity, attribute] terms `they, among, most, unstable` statement `They are among the most unstable of spirits and have little to no semblance of intellect.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0042-0006` [identity] terms `will-o-wisp, spirit, light` statement `Will-O-Wisp is a spirit of light.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.181-184` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, suggest`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.181-184` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-4-spirits.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-5-titans` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0043-0031` [identity] terms `yeti, titan, two, half` statement `The yeti is a titan, about two and a half meters tall, that is rarely seen in cold, snowy regions, and its body is covered in thick white...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0043-0034` [limitation] terms `characters, cannot, move, freely` statement `Characters cannot move freely in deep snow areas such as those where the yeti lives.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0043-0033` [relationship, identity] terms `they, not, attack, very` statement `They are not very ferocious and do not actively attack humans, but they do seem to attack when they're hungry-- in cold areas with deep snow, it's not...` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.185-187` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, suggest`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.185-187` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-5-titans.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-6-mythical-beasts-and-magical-beasts-part-1` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0044-0013` [relationship, identity] terms `ceiling, hanger, magical, beast` statement `A ceiling hanger is a magical beast with arms of different sizes on its left and right sides.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0044-0016` [quantitative] terms `attacking, prey, lower, body` statement `When attacking prey, this lower body stretches, reaching a length of 6 meters.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0044-0015` [relationship] terms `sticking, lower, body, ceiling` statement `By sticking with this lower body, a ceiling hanger can also move along walls and ceilings.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.188-198` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.188-198` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-6-mythical-beasts-and-magical-beasts-part-1.md`

- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-6-mythical-beasts-and-magical-beasts-part-3` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0046-0015` [limitation, quantitative, relationship, identity] terms `most, feared, beast, second` statement `The most feared beast second only to the dragon, the roc is a gigantic bird resembling an eagle, with a wingspan of 20 meters.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0046-0016` [uncertainty, identity] terms `once, every, few, years` statement `Once every few years, they may be swept close to the continent by the wind, and are the subject of gossip among sailors.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0046-0017` [quantitative, relationship, identity] terms `roc, talons, alone, over` statement `A roc's talons alone are over 1 meter long, and it can lift and carry humansized animals with one foot, or a cow or horse with both feet.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.188-198` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.188-198` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-6-mythical-beasts-and-magical-beasts-part-3.md`

- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-9-undead-part-1` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0051-0042` [identity] terms `these, undead, created, lost` statement `These are undead created by the lost spell _Create Buau Zombie_ .`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0051-0043` [unlabeled] terms `unlike, normal, zombies, they` statement `Unlike normal zombies, they possess intellect, albeit low, and can make independent decisions and follow the commands of their creator.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0051-0044` [unlabeled] terms `therefore, they, will, best` statement `Therefore, they will do their best to obey even abstract commands (for example, sneak into a house and steal something, etc.).` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.214-221` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, might, possible, suggest, unknown`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.214-221` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-9-undead-part-1.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-9-undead-part-2` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0052-0011` [identity] terms `knight, undead, ghost, appears` statement `An undead knight is a ghost that appears in the form of a knight wearing armor, holding a large two-handed sword.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0052-0021` [limitation] terms `undead, knights, cannot, wounded` statement `Undead knights cannot be wounded by ordinary weapons.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0052-0029` [uncertainty] terms `for, reason, undead, knight` statement `For this reason, an undead knight may be accompanied by multiple wights.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0052-0028` [provenance, quantitative, identity] terms `will, those, whose, mental` statement `Those whose mental power is reduced to 0 due to damage from an undead knight's weapons or gaze will die instantly and will be resurrected as a wight...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0052-0023` [unlabeled] terms `damage, undead, knight, attack` statement `Also, if an undead knight's attack hits, the character will suffer damage to their mental power in addition to normal damage.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.214-221` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, unknown`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.214-221` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-2-9-undead-part-2.md`

- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-5-monster-level-and-adventurer-level` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0056-0021` [identity] terms `level, character, monster, score` statement `A monster levelŌĆÖs score is basically set so that if it's equal to a character's adventurer level, the character will be stronger.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0056-0014` [quantitative, function] terms `scores, monster, here, order` statement `Here, in order to represent monsters such as featherfolk, mermen, dark elves, noble lizardmen, and centaurs as characters, weŌĆÖll show how to calculate the sub-ability scores of each...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0056-0027` [limitation, quantitative] terms `level, crisis, novice, adventurer` statement `Cue the novice adventurers.| |Level 5-6|Too spicy for a novice adventurer| |Level 7-8|A difcult enemy unless you're a moderate adventurer| |Level 9-10|A city-wide crisis| |Level 11-13|A nation-wide crisis|...`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0056-0022` [quantitative, comparison] terms `they, levels, higher, you` statement `If they're 1-2 levels higher, youŌĆÖll be evenly matched, and if theyŌĆÖre 3 levels higher, theyŌĆÖll be a formidable enemy.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0056-0023` [quantitative, comparison] terms `level, will, appear, introducing` statement `When introducing them into a scenario, assume that enemies of the same or 1 level lower than a character's adventurer level will appear frequently, while an enemy of...` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.234-234` in every bullet_text; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.234-234` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-5-monster-level-and-adventurer-level.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-6-handling-monster-special-abilities` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0057-0001` [uncertainty, negative-evidence, comparison, identity] terms `particular, monsters, magic, without` statement `In particular, monsters that use magic are, without exception, more formidable enemies than their level suggests.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0057-0003` [method, quantitative, attribute] terms `monster, level, scale, event` statement `The monster level of a monster can also be used as a scale to express the size of an event ( _Table 13-3: Monster Level/Event Scale Comparisons_ ).`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0057-0009` [relationship] terms `magic, monsters, ability, indicated` statement `Monsters with this ability can use magic at the indicated level and magic intensity (magic power).`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0057-0008` [unlabeled] terms `special, attacks, those, make` statement `**Special Attacks: Those That Make Mental Power Resistance Rolls**`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0057-0007` [unlabeled] terms `next, will, list, these` statement `Next, we will list these abilities and how to handle them ingame.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.235-240` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, might, possible, suggest`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.235-240` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-13-6-handling-monster-special-abilities.md`
- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-14-1-treasure-and-rewards-in-sword-world` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0058-0011` [provenance, identity] terms `some, larger, towns, there` statement `In some larger towns, there are shops that buy and sell these treasures from the ancient kingdom.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0058-0010` [uncertainty, limitation] terms `not, however, unlike, normal` statement `However, unlike normal treasures, itŌĆÖs not possible to appraise these goods unless you take them to a specialty shop, so they will likely not be purchased.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0058-0006` [unlabeled] terms `course, ancient, kingdom, was` statement `Of course, the ancient kingdom was a land where magic developed, so youŌĆÖll also find weapons, armor, treasure,`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0058-0005` [unlabeled] terms `many, treasures, found, ruins` statement `Many of the treasures found in the ruins of the ancient kingdom consist of handicrafts such as gems, precious metals, wares, works of art, etc.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0058-0001` [unlabeled] terms `think, adventurers, alecrast, basically` statement `Think of the adventurers of Alecrast as basically on an adventure to search for treasure lying untouched in the ruins of the ancient kingdom.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.241-242` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, might, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.241-242` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-14-1-treasure-and-rewards-in-sword-world.md`

- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-16-1-combat-in-which-monsters-roll-dice` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0062-0012` [provenance, relationship, procedure, identity] terms `you, see, way, dice` statement `As you can see from the way the dice are rolled, this is the process for a success roll with _monster's attack points -7_ as the _difficulty_ .`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0062-0020` [relationship, procedure, identity] terms `previous, section, process, for` statement `As in the previous section, this is the process for a success roll with _monster's evasion points -7_ as the _difficulty_ .`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0062-0003` [uncertainty] terms `however, some, game, masters` statement `However, some game masters may also want to roll the dice.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0062-0022` [requirement] terms `please, remember, game, master` statement `Please remember that the game master should be considered _first_ to roll the dice.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0062-0001` [unlabeled] terms `sword, world, rpg, monster` statement `In _Sword World RPG_ , the monster side (game master) rarely rolls dice during combat.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.261-264` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.261-264` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-16-1-combat-in-which-monsters-roll-dice.md`

- `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-16-7-throwing-multiple-darts-or-daggers` | plan_pages action `enrich` | PagePlan action `enrich-existing` | PageKind `source` | ResolvedPageBodyContract `source-summary` | SourceSummaryPlan selected claims (use claim_id only in covered_source_claims): claim_id `source-claim-unit-0064-0018` [requirement, attribute] terms `same, all, objects, thrown` statement `All objects thrown at the same time must have the _same weight_ (required strength).`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0064-0016` [uncertainty] terms `possible, throw, multiple, darts` statement `ItŌĆÖs possible to throw **multiple** darts or daggers at once.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0064-0022` [unlabeled] terms `you, cannot, choose, multiple` statement `You _cannot_ choose _multiple_ targets.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0064-0021` [quantitative] terms `for, example, you, throw` statement `For example, if you throw _three_ at the same time, thatŌĆÖll incur a -3 penalty to your attack power for each hit check.`; claim_id `source-claim-unit-0064-0020` [unlabeled] terms `number, thrown, same, time` statement `The _number_ thrown at the same time becomes a _penalty_ to attack power.` | constraints: put citation `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.268-273` in every bullet_text; include one uncertainty term `may, possible`; stay under 220 words | sources `raw/Sword World RPG - Complete Edition.pdf p.268-273` | path `sword-world-rpg-complete-edition-16-7-throwing-multiple-darts-or-daggers.md`
